title: 9 Natural Herbal Creams to Combat Heartburn and Indigestion

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for heartburn because it helps to soothe the lining of the esophagus and reduce inflammation that can cause heartburn.
Shogaol is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the esophagus and ease digestion, thereby reducing the burning sensation caused by heartburn.

Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce irritation caused by stomach acid, thereby providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Zingiberene is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by heartburn.
Gingerols are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, which can ease the burning sensation and discomfort caused by heartburn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy for heartburn. The active compound in ginger, called gingerol, helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach. When you eat, food moves down your esophagus and into your stomach. If the ring of muscle at the top of your stomach, called the lower esophageal sphincter, doesn't close tightly enough, stomach acid can leak back up into your esophagus, causing heartburn. Ginger helps to calm this muscle and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up. It also helps to speed up digestion, so food moves through your system more quickly. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach muscles, ginger cream can provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms, such as a burning sensation in the chest and difficulty swallowing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, start by gathering a few simple ingredients. You'll need 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel, which you can extract from an Aloe vera leaf by cutting it open and scooping out the clear gel inside. Mix the aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil in a small bowl. Add 10 drops of aloe vera essential oil and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce heartburn symptoms. Stir all the ingredients well until they're fully combined, then pour the mixture into a small glass jar. Let the cream cool and harden completely, which may take a few hours, before applying it to the affected area as needed. This Aloe vera herbal cream can help calm the stomach and reduce heartburn symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, calm the muscles that cause stomach acid to flow up, and soothe the lining of the esophagus to prevent burning sensations.
Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation, making it easier for food to pass through the digestive system without causing discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for heartburn because they help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the stomach to digest food without causing discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream, also known as licorice root cream, is a natural remedy that can help with heartburn. This is because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you eat, the food goes into your stomach and can irritate the esophagus, causing heartburn. Glycyrrhizin in the licorice root cream can help soothe and calm the irritated esophagus, reducing the burning sensation. Additionally, licorice root has a protective effect on the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus, preventing further irritation and inflammation. This can lead to fewer and less severe heartburn episodes.  Glycyrrhizin also has a natural antacid effect, helping to neutralize stomach acid and reduce its production. This can also help to prevent heartburn. Furthermore, licorice root has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various digestive issues, including heartburn and indigestion. Its natural properties make it a popular alternative to prescription medications for heartburn.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil. First, combine the aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a bowl and mix well until they are fully incorporated. Next, add in the shea butter and beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler or in the microwave for 10-second intervals until the beeswax has melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before stirring in the peppermint essential oil. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before using it. To use, simply apply a small amount to your chest or abdomen as needed to help soothe and calm heartburn symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn symptoms.

Anethole in Aloe Vera is good for heartburn because it helps relax the muscles in the throat and esophagus, reducing inflammation and allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus less often.

Foeniculin is good for heartburn because it helps to calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation and increase mucus production in the esophagus, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and relieve symptoms of heartburn.
Tannins are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a plant used to make creams that help with heartburn. The active compounds in fennel work by reducing inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, which is often the cause of heartburn. Fennel also has natural antacids that help neutralize stomach acid, which can help to reduce the burning sensation associated with heartburn. Additionally, fennel creams can help to relax the muscles in the esophagus, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing heartburn. The soothing properties of fennel can also help to calm the stomach and reduce discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for heartburn.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera powder. First, extract the Aloe vera gel from a fresh Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the outer layer and scooping out the clear gel inside. Then, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt the coconut oil and shea butter together until smooth. Next, add the Aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera powder to the melted mixture and stir well to combine. Continue to heat the mixture for a few more minutes, until it reaches a temperature of around 160°F to 180°F, which is suitable for skin application. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool down to around 100°F to 110°F. Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to set and thicken. This Aloe vera herbal cream can be applied to the affected area to help soothe and calm heartburn symptoms, while the Aloe vera gel and aloe vera powder work to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach.
Flavonoids are good for heartburn because they can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and prevent acid reflux by acting as a natural barrier against stomach acid.
Saponins are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and prevent stomach acid from rising up into the esophagus, thereby soothing heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams can help with heartburn because they have soothing properties that calm the stomach and intestines. The gel from the aloe plant contains a substance called aloin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. This can help ease symptoms of heartburn, such as burning sensations and discomfort in the chest and throat. Aloe barbadensis creams can also help protect the lining of the stomach and intestines, making it harder for acid to cause damage. When applied topically as a cream, aloe barbadensis can also help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the throat and chest, making it easier to swallow and breathe. Additionally, aloe barbadensis creams may help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, you will need Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and baking soda. First, take a mature Aloe vera leaf and cut it to extract the clear gel inside. Next, mix 1/2 cup of the Aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil in a bowl until well combined. Then, add 1 teaspoon of baking soda and mix it into the mixture. Baking soda helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn. After mixing, pour the cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator. When you feel heartburn coming on, apply a small amount of the cream to your chest and stomach. The Aloe vera and shea butter will soothe and calm the skin, while the coconut oil and baking soda will help to reduce inflammation and neutralize acid.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can cause heartburn symptoms to decrease.
Menthol is good for heartburn because it helps to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, thereby providing quick relief from burning sensations in the chest.
Luteolin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, which can ease the burning sensation caused by acid reflux.
Quercetin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, which can ease the pain and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is often used to help with heartburn. The main reason for this is that peppermint has a soothing effect on the stomach and the digestive system. When we eat, our stomach works to break down food into smaller parts. However, sometimes this process can be too slow or too fast, leading to stomach acid building up and causing heartburn. Peppermint oil, which is often found in creams and ointments, can help relax the muscles in the stomach and ease digestion. This can help reduce the amount of stomach acid produced and ease the burning feeling associated with heartburn. Additionally, peppermint has a natural cooling effect, which can also provide relief from the discomfort and pain of heartburn. When applied topically as a cream, peppermint can be absorbed through the skin and into the body, where it can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and provide quick relief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, you'll need to combine Aloe vera gel with other soothing ingredients. Start by extracting the clear gel from an Aloe vera leaf, making sure to only take the clear inner part and not the yellow outer layer. Then, in a bowl, mix together 1/4 cup of the Aloe vera gel with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, stirring until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and add in 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and 10 drops of calendula essential oil, which are known to help soothe heartburn and digestive issues. Stir well and pour the mixture into a glass jar. Let it cool and solidify before applying it to your chest or stomach area to help calm heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Mucilages in Aloe Vera help to calm down the burning sensation in the esophagus by forming a protective barrier that soothes and reduces inflammation, providing relief from the symptoms of heartburn.
Tannins are good for heartburn because they help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, calm acid production, and soothe the digestive tract to provide relief from heartburn symptoms.
Saponins are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
Polysaccharides are good for heartburn because they help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with heartburn. It contains marshmallow root extract, which is known for its soothing and protective properties. When you have heartburn, the acid in your stomach can irritate the lining of your esophagus, causing discomfort and pain. The marshmallow root in Althaea officinalis cream creates a protective barrier on the lining of the esophagus, preventing the acid from irritating it. This helps to reduce inflammation and ease the discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, the cream can also help to neutralize the acid and reduce its production, making it an effective treatment for heartburn symptoms. The natural ingredients in Althaea officinalis cream make it a safer alternative to over-the-counter medications, which can have harsh side effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, start by mixing 1 cup of aloe vera gel with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a bowl. You can extract the aloe vera gel from an aloe vera leaf by cutting off the green skin and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, add 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and 2 tablespoons of calendula oil to the bowl. Calendula oil is a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe the digestive system. Then, add 2 teaspoons of baking soda to the mixture, as it helps neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn symptoms. Mix all the ingredients together until you get a smooth and even consistency. Transfer the mixture to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator. Before applying the cream, gently massage it onto the stomach area to help soothe heartburn symptoms and promote digestion. You can apply it 2-3 times a day as needed. Note: Before using any new skincare or herbal remedy, do a patch test to ensure you're not allergic to any of the ingredients.
remedies.image_alt: Althaea officinalis herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they help calm down the stomach and reduce inflammation, which in turn eases the burning sensation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux.

Flavonoids are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, prevent acid reflux, and strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up.

remedies.remedy_desc: Alpinia galanga cream is used to help with heartburn. This cream has ingredients that help to reduce acid in the stomach. The main ingredient, Galangal, is a plant-based extract that has been used for years in traditional medicine. It helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, allowing food to move through the digestive system more easily. This reduces the amount of acid that is produced, which in turn helps to prevent heartburn. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. When applied to the stomach area, the cream can provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms, such as burning and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, you will need some basic ingredients and a few simple steps. First, you will need to harvest or buy aloe vera gel from a fresh aloe vera leaf, and then mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:3 ratio. For example, if you have 100 grams of aloe vera gel, you will need to mix it with 300 grams of the carrier oil. Next, add some soothing herbs like calendula, chamomile, or licorice root to the mixture, and mix well. You can also add some essential oils like peppermint or ginger to give the cream a pleasant smell and extra benefits. After mixing all the ingredients together, pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once the cream has solidified, you can apply it to your skin, preferably on the stomach area where you feel the heartburn. The aloe vera gel will help soothe and calm the skin, while the herbs and essential oils will provide extra relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Alpinia galanga herbal creams for heartburn

Quinidine is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system and relaxes the muscles that can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Quinic acid helps with heartburn because it reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, soothing the esophagus and calming the burning sensation associated with acid reflux.
Tannins are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause heartburn.
Alkaloids are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing the symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with heartburn. The cream is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, which contains a compound called quinine. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by heartburn. When you apply the cream to your skin, the quinine is absorbed into your body and can help calm the muscles in the esophagus that can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the throat. This can provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms such as burning sensations, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis cream can help reduce acid production in the stomach, further alleviating heartburn symptoms. By reducing inflammation and acid production, this natural cream can provide effective relief from heartburn without the side effects of prescription medications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream for heartburn relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: aloe vera gel, coconut oil, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and baking soda. First, carefully extract the gel from a fresh aloe vera leaf and mix it with a small amount of water to create a smooth paste. Next, melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, then let it cool down. Once cooled, combine the aloe vera paste, melted oil mixture, and vitamin E oil in a bowl and mix well until you get a smooth cream. Add a small amount of baking soda to help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation. Transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to allow it to thicken and set. When ready to use, simply apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe and calm heartburn symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for heartburn

Aloin helps with heartburn because it reduces inflammation in the esophagus and soothes the lining, preventing acid reflux and discomfort.
Saponins are good for heartburn because they help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera cream can help with heartburn because it has soothing properties that calm the digestive system. When you eat something that causes heartburn, it can irritate the lining of your esophagus and throat, leading to discomfort and pain. Aloe vera cream, when applied topically to the affected area, can help reduce inflammation and ease the burning sensation. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera also help to calm the digestive system, reducing the production of stomach acid that causes heartburn. Additionally, aloe vera cream contains compounds that promote healing and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, helping to prevent further irritation and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with heartburn, start by washing your hands and aloe vera leaves thoroughly with soap and water. Then, remove the outer skin of the aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces. Next, blend the aloe vera pieces in a blender or food processor until smooth. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining pulp or fibers. Now, mix 2 tablespoons of the aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil or shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil in a small bowl. Add a pinch of baking soda to help neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn symptoms. Finally, transfer the mixture to a small jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. When needed, apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in. Note: If you have sensitive skin, do a patch test before using the cream.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for heartburn

intro_desc: Herbal creams can provide relief for heartburn sufferers due to their soothing and protective properties. One herb, Zingiber officinale (ginger), has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. This helps ease the burning sensation associated with heartburn. Another herb, Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root), has antacid properties that neutralize stomach acid and provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms.  Heartburn occurs when stomach acid flows back up into the esophagus, causing discomfort and pain. If left untreated, it can lead to complications such as esophagitis, stomach ulcers, and even respiratory problems. Fixing heartburn issues is essential to prevent these complications and improve quality of life. By using herbal creams containing ginger and licorice root, individuals can alleviate symptoms and enjoy everyday activities without discomfort. Improved digestion and reduced heartburn frequency also contribute to better sleep, reduced anxiety, and increased energy levels.
supplementary_best: To remedy heartburn, a combination of Aloe barbadensis, Foeniculum vulgare, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is the most effective. Aloe barbadensis, also known as Aloe vera, soothes and calms the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to heal and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, has natural antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent gas and bloating. This helps to reduce symptoms of heartburn, such as discomfort and pain. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. It also helps to protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from acid damage. Together, these three herbs work synergistically to provide fast and effective relief from heartburn symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have heartburn, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing 'Valeriana officinalis' (valerian root). This herb can relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce the pressure that helps to keep stomach acid from moving up into the esophagus. While this might seem helpful, it can actually make heartburn worse by reducing the body's ability to prevent acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Another herb to avoid is 'Piper methysticum' (kava root). Kava has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can worsen heartburn symptoms by allowing stomach acid to flow more easily into the esophagus.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies can help with heartburn besides herbal creams. Aloe vera juice is one such remedy. When you drink aloe vera juice, it helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera also help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, aloe vera juice can help to break down food in the stomach, making it easier to digest and reducing the likelihood of heartburn.  Ginger has also been shown to help with heartburn. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, preventing heartburn. Ginger can also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to move through the digestive system more smoothly and reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Ginger can be consumed as tea, added to food, or taken as a supplement.  Another natural remedy that can help with heartburn is apple cider vinegar. Apple cider vinegar can help to balance the pH levels in the stomach, reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Apple cider vinegar can also help to break down food in the stomach, making it easier to digest and reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Some people also drink apple cider vinegar before meals to help prevent heartburn.  Probiotics are also a natural remedy that can help with heartburn. Probiotics are live bacteria and yeast that are beneficial for gut health. They can help to balance the gut microbiome, reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach and esophagus. Probiotics can also help to break down food in the stomach, making it easier to digest and reducing the likelihood of heartburn.  Baking soda can also help with heartburn. When you mix baking soda with water, it creates a natural antacid that can help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn. Baking soda can also help to break down food in the stomach, making it easier to digest and reducing the likelihood of heartburn. However, it's essential to note that baking soda should be used in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes in the body.  Some people also find relief from heartburn with the help of slippery elm. Slippery elm is a natural demulcent that can help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn. Slippery elm can also help to protect the stomach lining from the effects of stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of heartburn.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have heartburn often experience other ailments like bloating, constipation, and indigestion. These problems are usually caused by the same thing: stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus.  A natural remedy for these issues is the herb ginger. Ginger has been used for centuries to calm stomach problems. It works by reducing inflammation in the stomach and improving digestion. This helps to break down food properly and prevents acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.  Another herb that can help is slippery elm. It works by coating the stomach lining with a protective layer, which prevents acid from irritating the esophagus. This can help to reduce heartburn and other stomach problems.  Licorice root is also a helpful remedy. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an irritated stomach and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. This can help to calm heartburn and other stomach issues.  Dandelion root can also help to heal these ailments. It works by stimulating digestion and improving the way the body removes waste. This can help to prevent constipation and reduce bloating.  Peppermint oil can also be used to help soothe stomach problems. It works by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and improving digestion. This can help to reduce heartburn and other stomach issues.  All these herbs work by improving digestion, reducing inflammation, and protecting the stomach lining. This can help to prevent acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reduce heartburn and other stomach problems.
